route_pack_result.read: Torch sort-based route prepacking is comfortably below the sparse-atomic dKV wall on the real layer-2 S4096 LI route, so prepacking is viable as a first group-owned accumulator input contract.
route_pack_result.important_caveat: This is route metadata only. It does not time dKV contribution math or local/group-owned accumulation.

decision.promote_input_contract: Use pair_pos_sorted + group_ids_sorted + token_panel_starts sorted by (seq_tile, group_panel, token) as the first runtime input contract for the group-owned dKV accumulator.
decision.first_accumulator_rung: token_block=32, group_panel=256 remains the safer S128/S512 bring-up because it matches the prior 32-token target window; token_block=64, group_panel=256/512 is the higher-write-reduction follow-up once the accumulator body is stable.
decision.reject: Do not prototype a naive group-panel kernel that rescans top-k inside the accumulator; prepack is cheap enough to use or fuse.

next_kernel_gate: Build a HIP/CK/rocWMMA accumulator that consumes the prepacked route list and computes K16 contribution tiles without per-contribution scalar local-table overhead.
S128 parity against the shared-KV dKV reference.
S512 timing against the active-list sparse-atomic gate.
S4096 timing must beat the 8.0-8.8 ms sparse-atomic dKV slice including or explicitly accounting for route-pack cost.
